data_in.description: Query chemical list (ethyl hexanoate, methyl salicylate, octanal, undecane)
Restricted type library CSV with 5 chemical type sets (A–E)

data_out.description: Structural match results dataframe showing query chemicals vs. type categories with match outcome (No, ~, Yes) and matched compound identifier (e.g., CMP1, CMP2)
Best compound match mapping showing which compound in each type matched each query chemical (CMP identifier or blank if no match)

domain_knowledge: Structural match scores ≥0.95 indicate high similarity; scores 0.85–0.95 indicate moderate similarity (~); scores below 0.85 or no match indicate dissimilarity.
fmcsR performs flexible maximum common substructure (FMCS) analysis to quantify atomic and functional-group overlap between compounds.
Isobutyl hexanoate (Butanoate ester with 4-carbon branched alkyl group) shares a hexanoate core ester functionality with ethyl hexanoate, explaining their high structural similarity.
ChemmineR and webchem retrieve SMILES strings and molecular fingerprints from PubChem and other chemical databases for automated structural comparison.
CMP indexing in output (CMP1 = first compound in set, CMP2 = second compound) allows users to trace which library compound matched the query chemical.

verify 4-set restricted chemical library file exists and is loadable in R (format: CSV or RData)
script_runs: execute categorate() with structural-match condition on 4-set library; verify output is a dataframe with no errors
output includes rows for Ethyl hexanoate and isobutyl hexanoate
output dataframe contains a column indicating structural similarity or classification type (e.g., 'Type', 'structural_class', 'match_type')
value in dataframe row for Ethyl hexanoate matches value in row for isobutyl hexanoate in the structural similarity/type column
output dataframe row(s) contain 'Type D' or equivalent classification label as specified in METRIC-structural-match-ethylhex-typeD
expert_review: expert_review: assess whether the structural similarity metric and Type D classification are chemically defensible for the Ethyl hexanoate–isobutyl hexanoate pair (both are aliphatic esters; isobutyl vs. ethyl ester groups differ only in branching of the alcohol moiety)

expert_review: confirm that the 4-set restricted chemical library composition and molecular structure definitions are appropriate for testing structural-match functionality and consistent with METRIC-structural-match-ethylhex-typeD

cmd: library(ChemmineR); library(fmcsR); library(webchem); query_chemicals <- read.csv('{inputs.query_chemicals}'); type_library <- read.csv('{inputs.type_library}'); results <- categorate(query_chemicals, type_library); print(results); write.csv(results, 'categorate_output_{task_id}.csv', row.names=FALSE)
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

expected_outputs: Structural match results dataframe showing query chemicals vs. type categories with match outcome (No, ~, Yes) and matched compound identifier (e.g., CMP1, CMP2)
Best compound match mapping showing which compound in each type matched each query chemical (CMP identifier or blank if no match)
finding: The categorate() function is designed to access categorical data for searched chemicals, enabling structural matching and similarity assessment across chemical compounds.
inputs: Query chemical list (ethyl hexanoate, methyl salicylate, octanal, undecane)
Restricted type library CSV with 5 chemical type sets (A–E)
methodology_summary: Load query chemicals (ethyl hexanoate, methyl salicylate, octanal, undecane) and restricted type library with 5 structural categories (A–E).
Invoke categorate() to access cheminformatics packages (ChemmineR, fmcsR, webchem) and compute structural fingerprints and similarity scores.
Extract structural match outcome matrix (Yes/~/No) where Yes ≥0.95, ~ = 0.85–0.95, No <0.85 or no match.
Extract best-match compound identifier (CMP1, CMP2, ...) for each query chemical in each type category.
Validation: Verify that ethyl hexanoate row in Type D column shows 'CMP2' (isobutyl hexanoate, 2nd compound in Type D set) with match score ≥0.95 in source match-score matrix.
missing_information: No changelog documenting updates, versions, or changes to the categorate() function, the 4-set chemical library, or the structural-match condition since initial release.
research_question: Does the categorate() function correctly identify structurally similar compounds (Ethyl hexanoate and isobutyl hexanoate) when applied under structural-match conditions using a restricted chemical library?

task_description: Execute the categorate() function on a restricted 4-set chemical library to perform structural matching against query chemicals, and verify that the output correctly identifies ethyl hexanoate as structurally similar (match ≥0.95) to isobutyl hexanoate in Type D. Produce a dataframe showing structural match results with compound identifiers.

task_inputs.label: List of query chemicals for structural matching
Restricted chemical library with 5 structural type sets

task_objective: Run categorate() on the 4-set restricted library under structural-match conditions and validate that the output dataframe correctly classifies Ethyl hexanoate as Type D (structurally similar to isobutyl hexanoate).

task_outputs.label: Match outcome dataframe (Yes/~/No per chemical–type pair)
Compound identifier mapping (CMP1, CMP2, etc.) for matched pairs

title: Reproduce the categorate output dataframe including structural similarity results for the restricted chemical library

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Structural match results dataframe showing query chemicals vs. type categories with match outcome (No, ~, Yes) and matched compound identifier (e.g., CMP1, CMP2), Best compound match mapping showing which compound in each type matched each query chemical (CMP identifier or blank if no match).
workflow_description: 1. Load the query chemicals (ethyl hexanoate, methyl salicylate, octanal, undecane) and the restricted type library (Types A–E) into R. 2. Execute categorate() to access cheminformatics packages (ChemmineR, fmcsR, webchem) for structural comparison and atomic feature analysis against each chemical type set. 3. Extract the BestChemMatch dataframe from categorate() output, filtering for match scores and identifying the best-matched compound in each type for each query chemical. 4. Verify that ethyl hexanoate shows a structural match score ≥0.95 to isobutyl hexanoate (Type D compound 2) and that the output table displays 'CMP2' in the Type D column for ethyl hexanoate.
